lyrics:
Dirk wore the name like a borrowed coat
It fit him fine, but scratched his throat
He walked the floor but didn't see
What Mom and Pop had meant to be
The numbers whispered, "You're not enough"
The charts were flat, the margins tough
He craved much more than pride and steel
He needed someone to make it real

Then one gray morning at his desk
McKay appeared with a golden slide
Said, "Legacy's nice-but scale's your guide"
He spoke of vision, teams, and flow
Of agile minds and vertical growth
He said, "The line's not profit-it's thought"
"Your people fail when they think what they're not"
"Culture's a lever. Use it right."
"Above the line, you win the fight."

(Above the line) - you lead with grace
(Below the line) - you lose your place
(Above the line) - you show you care
(Below the line) - you're unaware
(You're not fired) - you're out-placed
(You're not sad) - you're misaligned
(You're not angry) - you're just Below the Line

We filled out forms with perfect grace
Learned how to smile at our own pace
We played the game, we joined the call
Said nothing true, said nothing at all
They tracked our joy in colored charts
Gave us a score to match our hearts
And when we cried, they changed the frame-
"You're not upset. You're just ashamed."

He heard the grumbles through the halls
The nervous laughs, the distant calls
He read the note from Mom last night-
"Don't lose the heart. Don't lose the light."
He asked McKay if things felt wrong
McKay just smiled and said, "Stay strong."
"This friction means the change is near-
The line divides the doubt from clear."

Word games are a coward's tool
(You're not tired) - you're unaligned
(You're not fired) - you've been out-placed
(You're not mad) - you're Below the Line
We used to be the name on steel
Now we're a number, masked as feel
We used to speak
Now we perform
The weather changed-
And we were the storm

(Above the line) - you lead with grace
(Below the line) - you lose your place
(Above the line) - you grow, you shine
(Below the line) - you're out of time
(You're not broken) - you're non-compliant
(You're not sad) - you're just defiant
(You're not worthy) - you're misaligned
(You're not hurting) - you're Below the Line

There used to be stew in the breakroom air
A joke, a name, a coat left there
A metal stamp that said, "I care"
But now the words just echo... square
Bent below the line…
persona:
## 4Castle

The Future of Rock, The Power of Harmony

**4Castle **is a genre-defying, five-piece supergroup under Maricopa Records, built on a foundation of powerful vocal harmonies, intricate musicianship, and fearless innovation. Rooted in rock, but unbound by limitations, their music fuses elements of progressive rock, funk, Latin rhythms, blues, and folk, delivering a sound that is both technically complex and emotionally resonant.

### **A Band Like No Other**

Each member of **4Castle **brings a distinct voice-both musically and vocally-to the mix:
🎸 **Raven Cortez**(Lead Guitar, Vocals) – A fiery virtuoso with Latin and blues influences, weaving face-melting solos into every performance.
🎸 **Skylar “Sky” Monroe**(Rhythm Guitar, Vocals) – The poetic soul of the band, crafting melodic foundations and lyrically rich storytelling.
🎹 **Jonah Vega**(Keyboards, Vocals) – The sonic architect, layering psychedelic synths, Hammond organ swells, and cinematic orchestrations.
🎸 **Malcolm “Mack” Bishop**(Bass, Vocals) – The groove master, blending funk, jazz, and progressive rock into fluid, deep-pocket basslines.
🥁 **Eli “Fox” Foster **(Drums, Vocals) – The wild heartbeat, pushing the band forward with thunderous fills, polyrhythmic grooves, and explosive energy.

### **What Defines 4Castle?**

- **Multi-Layered Vocal Harmonies**– All five members **sing**, creating **rich, choral-like textures**that elevate every song.

- **Complex Arrangements & Bold Songwriting**– Each track is an **immersive journey**, balancing **technical mastery with raw emotion**.

- **Rhythmic Power & Innovation**– From **tight funk grooves to shifting time signatures**, their music **moves you-literally and figuratively**.

- **Lyrically Challenging Yet Accessible**– Their songs explore **deep themes of life, change, and resilience**, wrapped in **memorable hooks and melodies**.

### **4Castle’s Sound**

Imagine putting Emerson, Lake & Palmer, Heart, Led Zeppelin, Sly Stone, and Carlos Santana in a blender. The result? **4Castle**. Their music pays homage to the greats while pushing rock’s boundaries into the future.

### **Join the Journey**

With Maricopa Records, **4Castle **is set to redefine what a modern rock band can be. Expect genre-bending albums, electrifying performances, and music that resonates across generations.

🚀 Follow **4Castle **as they build their legacy-one song, one harmony, and one unforgettable moment at a time.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Cmxf7xuTHzf7UahMK2xJh)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/4castle/1801052673)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/5-rooks-in-a-dark-setting-1.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track delivers a dark, dystopian, and tense electronic atmosphere, driving a steady, mid-energy pulse with its heavy synth patterns and punchy, industrial percussion. The vocals, delivered in a rhythmic, spoken-word style with a deep and detached tone, sit upfront in the mix, surrounded by an intricate web of deep sub-bass and crisp percussive textures. The production quality is highly polished, creating a dense yet spacious field where the elements are tightly layered, enhancing the claustrophobic and intense mood of the piece. A defining element of the track is the robotic, call-and-response vocal delivery of "above the line, below the line," which introduces a chilling, corporate-dystopian sci-fi quality to the music.

review.review_text: This is 4Castle working in a colder mode than usual, trading their harmony stacks for a tense, dystopian pulse: a steady 99 BPM in D major, with detached spoken-word vocals riding heavy synths and industrial percussion. The conceit is a corporate satire that actually bites, and the call-and-response hook does the heavy lifting, that robotic 'You're not fired, you're out-placed' landing with real menace, like Roger Waters narrating an HR onboarding video. The best writing is the small human detail underneath the jargon: 'There used to be stew in the breakroom air, a joke, a name, a coat left there' grounds the whole thing before the satire swallows it. At nearly five minutes the central trick wears a little thin by the final chorus, and the steady mid-energy pulse never quite breaks into release, but the idea is sharp enough to carry it. A highlight, and one that rewards a close listen to the lyrics.
anchors_used: 'There used to be stew in the breakroom air'
